description: Running a global build/test command for SQL instead of the database client and migration runner misses the project-local config and reports misleading results.
body: Failure mode: a generic root-level command is run for SQL work instead of the database client and migration runner, so project-local configuration, lockfiles, and scripts are ignored and the output does not reflect the real build. Detection signals: the command ignores the project manifest; results differ from CI; tooling versions or flags do not match the repo. Do not run a global or default command when the project declares its own SQL toolchain. Safer fallback: detect and run the database client and migration runner from the project root, verify the version matches the repo, and record the exact command and exit code.
